description: Sector overlay for South African medicines, medical devices, scheduled substances, pharmaceutical manufacturing/import/export, clinical trials, inspections, pharmacy practice, and regulated health-product documentation.

metadata.scope: South Africa-only sector overlay controls for Pharmaceuticals. Intended to be used with south_africa_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official South African statutes, regulators, guidance, and sector sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: Medicines and Related Substances Act, 1965 as amended
SAHPRA Inspectorate, Licencing and Regulatory Compliance
Pharmacy Act and South African Pharmacy Council framework

rules.title: SAHPRA registration or authorization evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for SAHPRA product registration, marketing authorization, section 21 authorization, licence, or permit evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
source_basis: Medicines and Related Substances Act / SAHPRA

rules.title: GMP or manufacturing/import licence evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for GMP, manufacturer licence, importer licence, wholesaler licence, inspection, or quality-system evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Clinical trial authorization or ethics evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for clinical trial approval, ethics approval, protocol, informed consent, or trial registration evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Pharmacovigilance or adverse-event reporting evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for adverse event, pharmacovigilance, safety report, product quality complaint, or risk-management evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Pharmacy premises or professional dispensing evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for pharmacy registration, premises licence, pharmacist, responsible pharmacist, dispensing, or SAPC evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
